title: practical english usage - Part 234
content:
We do not normally use full stops, question or exclamation marks to close We need more houses! Not one day, not next year - now!

Why do we try to reach the stars? What is it all for?

I looked out of the window. It was snowing again.

sentence has a capital letter.

(AmE exclamation points) are used to close sentences. VAT                    value added tax US   URGED   TO  PULL   OUT   OF  MIDDLE  EAST US                    the United States of America GOVERNMENT     URGED   TO  ACT  ON  POLLUTION urge             encourage UN  IN RED:  CANNOT   BALANCE  BUDGET UN                   the United Nations BRUSSELS     CRITICISES    UK  JAIL CONDITIONS UK                     the United Kingdom (of Great Britain and Northern Ireland) MORE   TROOPS     FOR   BORDER    AREA troops       soldiers JAILBREAK   TRIO   RECAPTURED trio             three people VICTORY     FOR   TORY    MODERATES Tory               Conservative IMPORTS    TOP  LAST   YEAR'S   FIGURES top               (verb) exceed TOP  BANKER  KIDNAPPED top               (adj) senior, most important QUAKE   TOLL   MAY   BE 5,000 toll               number killed THREAT    OF  FLU  EPIDEMIC threat       danger Written Texts   Section 26 grammar •  294  punctuation: colon British usage prefers a comma or no punctuation mark at all in this case.

I am writing to ...

in a business letter.

Americans usually put a colon after the opening salutation (Dear...) 5  letters In American English, colons are more often followed by capital letters.

Secondly, there is no guarantee that. ..

First of all, no complete budget has been drawn up.

My main objections are as follows: several complete sentences.

beginning of a quotation). However, this can happen if a colon is followed by In British English, it is unusual for a capital letter to follow a colon (except at the 4 capitals PUNCTUATION:     COLON A  colon can introduce a subdivision of a subject in a title or heading.

We need three kinds of support: economic, moral and political.

The main points are as follows: (1) . .., (2)..., (3) ....

A  colon can introduce a list.

There was a problem with the car: it was losing oil.

We decided not to go to Mexico: we had too little money.

A  colon (:) usually introduces an explanation or further details.

294 pun   ctua   tion   : col  on  __           __ not I asked her what time it was. (     ... what time-it-was?) We   do not use question marks after indirect questions (►  260).

Full stops can be used after many abbreviations (►  336). But they are often 2 abbreviations Because there are not enough houses.

People are sleeping out on the streets. In Britain. In the 21st century.

a full stop and capital letter.

However, sometimes we  can emphasise a clause or phrase by separating it with Why-I was upset?) not Did you understand why I was upset? During the late 1920s and early 1930s, the standard of living of the dock At that time the standard of living of the dock workers was slowly improving.

use a comma. Compare: If we  put long adverbial expressions before the basic sentence, we  often before and after the basic sentence 2 not (     Many of them were able to begin buying, their own homes.) Many of them were able to begin buying their own homes.
